ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Truffle Aglio Olio pasta; closest parent 'pasta dish' (FOODON_00002473) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Pasta, cooked) used as closest parent; no specific code for truffle aglio olio pasta exists in FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 229953004 'Pasta (food)' used as closest parent; no specific concept for truffle aglio olio pasta.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of Pasta Truffle Aglio Olio is approximately 350 g, comprising a full plate of spaghetti tossed in truffle-infused olive oil with garlic.

nutrient_source.title: Spaghetti with olive oil and garlic (Aglio e Olio), cooked — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods, adapted with truffle oil component
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central data for cooked spaghetti (FDC ID 20124), olive oil (FDC ID 04053), and garlic (FDC ID 11215), weighted to reflect a typical aglio olio formulation (approximately 65% pasta, 20% olive oil, 10% garlic, 5% truffle oil/shavings). Truffle oil is nutritionally similar to olive oil for macro purposes. Values represent a composite per-100g estimate. This is an adapted estimate; no exact USDA or HPB entry for Pasta Truffle Aglio Olio was found.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — spaghetti, white, boiled
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 48 is based on the University of Sydney GI Database value for white spaghetti, boiled (GI ~46–50, mean 48). Aglio olio preparation with olive oil does not substantially alter GI relative to plain boiled pasta. Truffle oil addition is nutritionally negligible for GI purposes. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (48 × 31.5 × 3.5) / 100 = 52.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~210 mg/100g; ~735 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that a restaurant serving of this dish may contribute approximately 735 mg sodium, representing about 32% of the recommended daily limit of 2300 mg; portion control and requesting reduced salt preparation are advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (GL ~53 per serving) from refined pasta carbohydrates
type2_diabetes.guidance: Persons with Type 2 Diabetes should note that a full restaurant serving carries a high glycemic load (~53) despite the low GI of pasta; reducing portion size to approximately 150–200 g and pairing with non-starchy vegetables can help moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Predominantly monounsaturated fat from olive oil; low saturated fat and zero cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The olive oil base provides a favourable monounsaturated fat profile (approximately 7.4 g/100g) with low saturated fat (1.5 g/100g) and no cholesterol, which is consistent with a heart-healthy dietary pattern when consumed in moderate portions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pasta Truffle Aglio Olio is an Italian-origin pasta dish served in Singapore's casual-dining and café restaurant segment. It consists of spaghetti or linguine cooked al dente and tossed in a generous quantity of extra-virgin olive oil infused with thinly sliced or minced garlic, dried chilli flakes, and black truffle or truffle oil. The dish is finished with fresh flat-leaf parsley and sometimes shaved Parmesan, though the version served at Flavours by Saute is typically dairy-free. The flavour profile is intensely savoury and aromatic, with the earthy, umami-rich character of truffle complementing the pungency of garlic and the fruity richness of olive oil. The texture is silky and lightly coated rather than saucy. Nutritionally, the dish is moderate in energy (~245 kcal/100g), relatively high in carbohydrates from the pasta (~31.5 g/100g), and rich in monounsaturated fats from olive oil (~7.4 g/100g). The GI is low (~48) due to the pasta matrix, though the glycemic load per full serving is high owing to portion size. Sodium content is moderate and warrants attention for individuals managing hypertension. The dish is suitable for vegetarians and vegans and is halal-friendly. It is not suitable for those with gluten intolerance. Clinically, it is best consumed in controlled portions by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension.
common_user_queries: Calories in pasta truffle aglio olio
Is truffle pasta good for diabetics?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Pasta Truffle Aglio Olio was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or any other approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted adaptation of USDA SR Legacy data for cooked spaghetti, olive oil, and garlic. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database value for white spaghetti. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central component data (cooked spaghetti FDC 20124, olive oil FDC 04053, garlic FDC 11215) weighted at approximately 65:20:10:5 (pasta:olive oil:garlic:truffle oil). No exact approved-source entry exists for this dish. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — olive oil and pasta contain negligible trans fats per USDA data). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — this is a plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients in the base formulation). vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero — this is a vegan dish with no animal-derived ingredients). 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this composite dish in the adapted source data. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source data for these plant-based components. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. 4. GI of 48 is an analogous estimate based on white spaghetti, boiled, from the University of Sydney GI Database; gi_category is 'low' (GI ≤ 55). GL per serving = (48 × 31.5 × 3.5) / 100 = 52.9. 5. Serving size of 350 g is estimated based on typical Singapore restaurant pasta portion. 6. Dietary flags: is_vegetarian and is_vegan set to true for the base formulation; individual restaurants may add Parmesan or other dairy, which would alter these flags. 7. Ontology codes use closest_parent match type as no exact codes exist for this specific dish. 8. This dish is served by Flavours by Saute - Funan on field survey data at SGD 21.50.
